title: crinum yuccioides
intro: Crinum yuccioides, also known as the "wild onion," is a type of bulbous perennial plant that belongs to the Amaryllidaceae family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antibacterial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant has been found to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, treating wounds and burns, and providing relief from respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its bulbs can also be used to make a medicinal oil.
